hero.subtitle1: 54 composable skills wired into a single
hero.social: Open source · MIT License · Works offline

problem.label: 01 / the problem
problem.title: AI agents are capable but undisciplined.
problem.card1Desc: They ship code that looks correct but breaks in production.
problem.card2Desc: They overwrite each other's work without noticing.
problem.card3Desc: They produce the same patterns regardless of context.
problem.metr: When developers used AI, they took 19% longer. Developers estimated AI saved them 20% of the time. Experts predicted 38% faster.
problem.conclusion: The gap between "capable" and "reliable" is not intelligence. It's process.

howitworks.label: 02 / how it works
howitworks.title: Six phases. Every time.
howitworks.subtitle: Every project follows the same disciplined flow. No shortcuts. No skipped steps.
howitworks.phase1Desc: Write the spec before any code. Interview requirements. Lock scope.
howitworks.phase2Desc: Break work into atomic tasks. Estimate effort. Identify dependencies.
howitworks.phase3Desc: Implement one slice at a time. Test each before expanding.
howitworks.phase4Desc: Run tests. Check builds. Verify behavior matches spec.
howitworks.phase5Desc: Code review. Security audit. Performance check. Quality gates.
howitworks.conclusion: No phase is optional. Every step has a skill. Every skill has a gate.

enforcement.label: 03 / enforcement
enforcement.title: Rules that depend on memory fail.
enforcement.titleAccent: Rules that depend on visible blocks succeed.
enforcement.level1Desc: Agent remembers the rules. Fails when context degrades.
enforcement.level2Desc: Agent presents DECISION POINT before every mutation. Agent can skip the presentation.
enforcement.level3Desc: Hook verifies integrity. Agent cannot bypass what it cannot forge.
enforcement.incidentRef: We broke our own rules three times in two days. Then we made breaking them impossible.
enforcement.conclusion: We learn from our own failures. Faster than competitors ship features.

philosophy.label: 04 / philosophy
philosophy.principle1: Quality emerges from explicit contracts, not implicit trust.
philosophy.principle2: Rules that depend on memory fail. Rules that depend on visible blocks succeed.
philosophy.principle3: A hook that accepts any token is a suggestion. A hook that verifies integrity is a gate.
philosophy.principle4: Speed is a side effect of precision, never the primary goal.
philosophy.principle5: Context is finite. Evict before you drown.
philosophy.principle6: "No, that's overcomplicated" is more valuable than blind compliance.
philosophy.principle7: We learn from our own failures faster than competitors ship features.
philosophy.compact: We turn AI coding agents from obedient servants into disciplined senior engineers.

compatible.label: 05 / compatible agents
compatible.title: Designed for OpenCode.
compatible.subtitle: One install. Any agent. Any stack.
compatible.stackDesc: Works with Node, Rust, Python, Go, Ruby, Dart, or any stack.
compatible.stackDetail: init-agents detects your stack automatically. Creates STACK_CONFIG.md.

quickstart.label: 07 / quick start
quickstart.title: Start in seconds.
quickstart.conclusion: Your agent now has 38 skills + 45 guides. Every project. Every time.

faq.label: 08 / faq
faq.title: Questions.
faq.a1: A framework of 38 composable skills that turn AI coding agents into disciplined senior engineers. It adds mechanical enforcement, not just prompts. Skills follow a 6-phase lifecycle: Define, Plan, Build, Verify, Review, Ship.
faq.q2: How is it different from other skill frameworks?
faq.a2: Three things: (1) Mechanical enforcement via SHA256 hash-bound tokens and 10 pre-commit gates. (2) The Guardian Pattern: DECISION POINT before every mutation. (3) Context engineering: 245 lines always-loaded (1.8% of 200K context). Other frameworks give you prompts. We give you process.
faq.a3: Designed for OpenCode. Portable to Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Kiro, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, and any agent that reads AGENTS.md. Stack-agnostic: works with Node, Rust, Python, Go, Ruby, Dart, or any stack.
faq.a4: Yes. MIT License. Open source. No subscriptions. No paid tiers. The installer is free, the skills are free, the enforcement is free.
faq.a5: Three levels: (1) Process rules: agent remembers. (2) Visible manifest: agent presents a decision point. (3) SHA256 hash-bound token: hook verifies integrity. The agent cannot bypass what it cannot forge. We built this after breaking our own rules three times in two days.
faq.a6: Yes. Skills are Markdown files in the `skills/` directory. Edit them, add new ones, remove ones you don't need. The framework is modular: use all 38 or pick the ones that matter to your workflow.

references.label: references
references.title: Sources
references.metrTitle: Becker, J., Rush, N., Barnes, E., & Rein, D. (2025).
references.metrPaper: Measuring the Impact of Early-2025 AI on Experienced Open-Source Developer Productivity.
